title: Goldman Sachs Should Benefit From Lower Interest Rates In 2024
text: Goldman Sachs shares have underperformed the broader market this year, but have surged over the past two months. The company has faced challenges due to its failed consumer efforts and higher interest rates, but is poised to grow earnings in 2024. Lower interest rates are expected to benefit Goldman Sachs by increasing financial sector activity, M&A advisory fees, and underwriting revenue growth.

title: Investor Michael Farr is back with his top 10 stocks he's buying for 2024, and the tilt is defensive
text: This year's list is a bit more defensive than in years past and has a focus on earnings growth.

title: Goldman's partnership is too much of a good thing
text: The hallowed Goldman Sachs (GS.N) partnership is an undeniable part of the Wall Street firm's cachet. For more than 150 years, the elite club has helped attract top-notch financiers. It is also too much of a good thing.

title: Ray Dalio keeps adding to this ETF, what's he thinking?

title: Goldman Sachs: Holding On For Dividend Income Growth And Capital Resilience
text: Goldman Sachs downgraded to Hold from previous Buy rating this summer. High share price vs long-term moving average, and weak revenue/earnings growth make this a lousy buy right now. Proven dividend income growth is a bright spot for a dividend investor, along with this firm being considered systemically critical and its vast capital strength.

title: Goldman (GS) Eyes Expansion of Credit Business in India
text: Goldman (GS) intends to expand its credit business operations in the flourishing market of India. Notably, it plans to broaden the range of its loan offerings and boost its currency trading in the country.

text: The Dogs of the Dow in 2023 are up an average of just 2.8%, lagging the market by the widest amount since 2006, per Bespoke Investment Group, as quoted on Investors Business Daily. But more importantly, the Dogs of the Dow trailed the market in four of the past five years, Bespoke found.

title: Lendbuzz advancing toward $2B IPO: report
text: Lenduzz has hired investment banks to assist it in putting on an initial public offering (IPO) of its shares that could value the auto loans provider at more than $2 billion, according to a report from Reuters.  Sources with knowledge of the matter told Reuters that the company hopes to raise between $300 million and $400 million from the offering.

title: Report: Goldman May See Pressure in Leaving Apple Partnership
text: Goldman Sachs is reportedly facing an uphill climb in exiting its Apple Card partnership. The Wall Street giant is expected to face pressure from possible issuers to lower the value of its stake to make the price more enticing, Reuters reported Monday (Dec. 18), citing sources familiar with the matter.

title: Are bank stocks poised for a rebound? Three picks for 2024
text: Bank stocks have lagged behind the broad market for most of 2023, and the failure of three large institutions early in the year may have left a sour taste in investors' mouths. But some bank stocks are attractively priced right now, and targeted investments in the sector may turn out to be lucrative in 2024.

title: Goldman Sachs faces rocky exit from Apple credit card partnership

title: China industrial growth fastest in almost two years
text: China has reported the fastest monthly expansion of industrial output in almost two years of 6.6%, while retail sales grew 10.1%, as the world's second-largest economy looks to continue its patchy recovery from the pandemic. The People's Bank of China also announced no change to rates, in line with market expectations, while injecting RMB 1.45 trillion of funds, which was well ahead of market expectations.

title: Bond prices rally for JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs and Bank of America as sentiment turns bullish on money-center banks
text: Corporate bonds from the big money-center banks are rallying in concert with their stock prices amid more bullish sentiment on Wall Street about potential interest-rate cuts in 2024.

title: WeightWatchers has a new $99 monthly plan — and it's targeting those on weight-loss drugs like Wegovy
text: The weight-loss platform is launching a new plan, the WeightWatchers GLP-1 Program, for those who are obese or overweight and taking any of the increasingly popular injectable drugs that help with curbing hunger, such as Ozempic, Wegovy and Saxenda. Such drugs are known as GLP-1 medications because they mimic a gut hormone known as GLP-1, which can reduce appetite.

title: These Stocks Have Gained The Most During Stock Market's Record Rally
text: The stock market's nearly two-month-long rally culminated this week with the Dow Jones Industrial Average hitting an all-time high, officially erasing all the losses associated with the spike in interest rates, with gains spread across a diverse group of stocks.

title: Banks less sensitive to rate cuts: Goldman analysts
text: Next year will be a transition period for European banks as the sector moves to a lower rate environment, according to analysts at Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS).   The current environment is characterised by flattish net interest income (NII), higher fees, manageable operating expenses (opex), and credit costs, the analysts said.

title: Goldman Sachs commodities-trading head to retire after 24 years
text: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. GS, -0.05% executive Ed Emerson plans to retire in 2024 after 24 years at the bank, according to an internal memo seen by MarketWatch. Emerson, who currently serves as head of global commodities, reportedly earned $100 million in the past three years, which surpassed the $77.5 million earned by Chief Executive David Solomon, according to reports.

title: A sudden volume spike in these 3 stocks could mean something big

title: Goldman Sachs trader to retire after making $100m in three years
text: The boss of Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) commodity business is reportedly set to "retire" at the age of 47 after earning more than $100 million in three years and becoming known as a vocal critic of the investment bank's leadership. An internal report said oil and metals trader Ed Emerson was “known to relentlessly argue his views and sometimes clash with superiors” and made his own “wary of dealing with him around bonus time" as he pushed for higher pay, cited in media reports.

title: Goldman Sachs Appoints Wells Fargo Executive Paul Camp to Head Transaction Banking
text: Goldman Sachs has reportedly appointed Wells Fargo executive Paul Camp as its new head of transaction banking. Camp will also become a Goldman partner, Reuters reported Monday (Dec. 11).

title: Goldman hires Wells Fargo executive as head of transactional banking
text: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. GS, +0.23% is hiring former Wells Fargo & Co. WFC, -0.14% executive Paul Camp as partner and head of transaction banking at the firm, according to an internal memo seen by MarketWatch. Camp most recently worked as head of global Treasury management at Wells Fargo.

title: Klarna's valuation still way below ambitious IPO target
text: Swedish buy-now-pay-later giant Klarna's volatile valuation has shot up again after major shareholder Creades reportedly increased the value of its stake by $11.3 million. According to Bloomberg, this suggests a valuation of $7.85 billion Klarna valuation, a 17% increase from Klarna's $6.7 billion valuation penned in July 2022 following an $800 million funding round.

title: December Dogs Of The Dow: 3 Buyable, 5 Watchable
text: "The Dow [adds a stock] if the company has an excellent reputation, demonstrates sustained growth, and is of interest to a large number of investors. Sector representation is also a consideration.” – Dow Jones & Co. The highest-yield 10 stocks are December's Dogs of the Dow: KO, AMGN, CSCO, GS, IBM, CVX, DOW, MMM, VZ, and WBA. title: My Top 10 High Yield Dividend Stocks For December 2023

title: Should You Invest in Goldman (GS) Based on Bullish Wall Street Views?
text: The recommendations of Wall Street analysts are often relied on by investors when deciding whether to buy, sell, or hold a stock. Media reports about these brokerage-firm-employed (or sell-side) analysts changing their ratings often affect a stock's price.
